subject: Physics
reference: JEE Main 2026 / National Testing Agency (NTA) / jeemain.nta.nic.in
notes: 30 questions | 120 marks | Section A: 20 MCQs, Section B: 10 Numericals (attempt any 5) | Based on NCERT Class 11 & 12

sub_topics: Physics, technology and society
SI units and fundamental and derived units
Dimensions of physical quantities and dimensional analysis
Least count, accuracy and precision of measuring instruments
Errors in measurement — absolute, relative, and percentage error
Significant figures and rounding off
Applications of dimensional analysis — checking equations and unit conversion
Frame of reference and types of motion
Motion in a straight line — position-time graph, speed and velocity
Uniform and non-uniform motion, average speed and instantaneous velocity
Uniformly accelerated motion — equations of motion
Position-time and velocity-time graphs
Relative velocity
Motion in a plane — projectile motion
Uniform circular motion — centripetal acceleration
Newton's first law of motion — concept of inertia
Newton's second law of motion — momentum and force
Newton's third law of motion
Impulse and its applications
Law of conservation of linear momentum
Equilibrium of concurrent forces
Static and kinetic friction — laws of friction
Rolling friction and its applications
Dynamics of uniform circular motion — banking of curves, circular motion on a flat road
Work done by a constant force and variable force
Kinetic energy and work-energy theorem
Power — definition and applications
Potential energy — gravitational and spring potential energy
Conservative and non-conservative forces
Elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions
Law of conservation of mechanical energy
Centre of mass of a two-particle system and a rigid body
Basic concepts of rotational motion
Moment of a force — torque
Angular momentum and its conservation
Moment of inertia — definition and physical significance
Radius of gyration
Theorems of parallel and perpendicular axes
Moment of inertia of uniform bodies (ring, disc, sphere, rod)
Equilibrium of rigid bodies
Rolling motion without slipping
Newton's law of universal gravitation
Gravitational constant G
Acceleration due to gravity and its variation with altitude, depth, and latitude
Gravitational potential energy
Gravitational potential
Escape velocity
Orbital velocity of satellites
Geostationary satellites and polar satellites
Kepler's laws of planetary motion
Elastic behaviour of solids — stress, strain, and Hooke's law
Stress-strain diagram — elastic moduli (Young's, Bulk, and Shear modulus)
Pressure in a fluid — Pascal's law and its applications
Buoyancy and Archimedes' principle
Viscosity — Stokes' law, terminal velocity, streamlined and turbulent flow
Bernoulli's principle and its applications
Surface energy and surface tension — drops, bubbles, and capillary action
Heat and temperature — thermal expansion of solids, liquids, and gases
Specific heat capacity, calorimetry
Change of state — latent heat
Heat transfer — conduction, convection, and radiation
Newton's law of cooling
Blackbody radiation — Stefan's law and Wien's displacement law
Thermal equilibrium and zeroth law of thermodynamics
First law of thermodynamics — internal energy, heat, and work
Isothermal and adiabatic processes
Second law of thermodynamics — reversible and irreversible processes
Carnot engine and its efficiency
Heat engines and refrigerators — coefficient of performance
Equation of state of a perfect gas
Kinetic theory of gases — assumptions and pressure of an ideal gas
Kinetic energy and temperature
RMS speed, mean speed, and most probable speed
Degrees of freedom and the law of equipartition of energy
Mean free path
Avogadro's number and Boltzmann constant
Periodic motion — period, frequency, displacement as a function of time
Simple harmonic motion (SHM) — definition, conditions, and equations
SHM of a spring — restoring force and force constant
Energy in SHM — kinetic and potential energy
Simple pendulum — derivation of time period
Damped and forced oscillations — resonance (conceptual)
Wave motion — transverse and longitudinal waves
Speed of a wave — relationship between wavelength, frequency, and velocity
Displacement relation in a progressive wave
Principle of superposition of waves — reflection of waves
Standing waves in strings and pipes — harmonics and overtones
Beats
Doppler effect
Electric charges — conservation of charge, Coulomb's law
Electric field — electric field lines, electric field due to a point charge
Electric flux and Gauss's theorem — applications to uniform charge distributions
Electric potential — potential due to a point charge, dipole, and system of charges
Equipotential surfaces
Electrical potential energy of a system of two point charges
Electric dipole — electric field and potential due to a dipole
Conductors and insulators — redistribution of charges
Dielectrics and polarisation
Capacitance — parallel plate capacitor with and without dielectric
Series and parallel combinations of capacitors
Energy stored in a capacitor
Van de Graaff generator (conceptual)
Electric current and drift velocity
Ohm's law — resistance and resistivity
Electrical energy and power
Series and parallel combinations of resistors
Temperature dependence of resistance
Internal resistance of a cell — EMF and terminal voltage
Kirchhoff's laws — loop and junction rules
Wheatstone bridge and metre bridge
Potentiometer — principle and applications (EMF comparison, internal resistance measurement)

major_topics.topic_name: Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
sub_topics: Biot-Savart law — magnetic field due to a current-carrying conductor
Ampere's circuital law and its applications (solenoid, toroid)
Force on a moving charge in a magnetic field — Lorentz force
Force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field
Force between two parallel current-carrying conductors
Torque on a current loop — magnetic dipole
Moving coil galvanometer — conversion to ammeter and voltmeter
Bar magnet — magnetic field lines, magnetic dipole moment
Earth's magnetic field — horizontal component, angle of declination and dip
Paramagnetism, diamagnetism, and ferromagnetism
Cyclotron (principle and working)
Electromagnetic induction — Faraday's laws
Lenz's law and conservation of energy
Self and mutual inductance
Energy stored in an inductor
Eddy currents and their applications
Alternating current — peak and RMS values
AC circuits with resistor, capacitor, and inductor
Series LCR circuit — impedance and phase relations
Resonance in AC circuits — quality factor
Power in AC circuits — power factor
Transformers — principle and efficiency
AC generator — working principle
Displacement current and its significance
Maxwell's equations (conceptual)
Electromagnetic waves — nature and properties
Electromagnetic spectrum — radio waves, microwaves, infrared, visible, ultraviolet, X-rays, gamma rays
Applications of electromagnetic waves in daily life and technology
Reflection of light — spherical mirrors and mirror formula
Refraction of light — Snell's law and refractive index
Total internal reflection and its applications (optical fibres)
Refraction at spherical surfaces and by lenses
Thin lens formula and lens maker's equation
Power of a lens
Combination of thin lenses in contact
Refraction and dispersion through a prism
Scattering of light — blue colour of sky and reddish appearance of sun at sunrise/sunset
Optical instruments — microscope, telescope
Wave optics — wavefront and Huygens' principle
Reflection and refraction using Huygens' principle
Young's double slit experiment — interference and fringe width
Coherent sources and conditions for sustained interference
Diffraction — single slit diffraction pattern
Polarisation — Brewster's law, polaroids and their uses

major_topics.topic_name: Dual Nature of Matter and Radiation
sub_topics: Photoelectric effect — Hertz and Lenard's observations
Einstein's photoelectric equation — particle nature of light
Matter waves — wave nature of particles, de Broglie relation
Davisson-Germer experiment
Particle nature of light — photon and its properties
Work function, threshold frequency, stopping potential
Alpha particle scattering — Rutherford's nuclear model
Bohr's model of the hydrogen atom — energy levels and spectral lines
Hydrogen spectrum — Lyman, Balmer, Paschen series
Atomic masses and composition of the nucleus
Size of the nucleus
Radioactivity — alpha, beta and gamma decay
Law of radioactive decay and half-life
Mass-energy equivalence — mass defect and binding energy
Nuclear reactions — fission and fusion
Energy released in nuclear fission and fusion
Semiconductors — energy band structure, intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors
p-type and n-type semiconductors
p-n junction — forward and reverse bias characteristics
Junction diode — half-wave and full-wave rectifiers
Zener diode as a voltage regulator
Transistor — PNP and NPN, action and characteristics
Transistor as an amplifier — common emitter configuration
Transistor as a switch
Logic gates — OR, AND, NOT, NAND, NOR gates
Boolean algebra and De Morgan's theorems (basic)
